title: California Unveils Initial Details of $200M Electric Vehicle Rebate Program
content: Californiaâ€™s governorâ€™s administration released the first details of a proposed $200 million electric vehicle (EV) rebate program as part of the 2026â€“27 state budget process. Under this plan, the state would work with automakers to offer point-of-sale rebates for first-time EV buyers, requiring participating manufacturers to match the state rebate dollar-for-dollar, effectively doubling the funding available. Program specifics â€” such as the rebate amounts for new and used EVs â€” will be finalized by the California Air Resources Board, with eligibility and implementation details to be developed in upcoming meetings and workshops. The official California Trailer Bill 1367 (published by the California Department of Finance) establishes a new zero-emission electric vehicle incentive program under the state Health and Safety Code using funds provided in the state budget. It directs the California Air Resources Board to enter into grant agreements with original equipment manufacturers to provide rebates for both new and used light-duty electric vehicles at the point of sale, provided the car is registered to California residents. The incentives are capped by federal price limits and require disclosure that a portion of the rebate is state-funded. This program is set to operate through September 1, 2031 before repealing in 2032.
author: Analysts Account
